source: AD&D 2nd Edition Arms & Equipment Guide (1991), Complete Fighter's Handbook (PHBR1)
description: Additional weapons beyond the PHB, including polearms, exotic weapons, eastern weapons, siege weapons, and historical weapons from the Arms & Equipment Guide
notes: Damage values are versus Small/Medium and Large creatures respectively. Speed factor affects initiative in combat.

polearms.description: Long pole weapons used primarily by infantry. Most require two hands. Polearms were critical military weapons of the medieval period.
general_rules: Most polearms require 10 ft of space to use effectively (5 ft in tight quarters with -2 to hit)
Polearms of 'set' type can be set against a charge for double damage on the first hit
Polearms can usually attack opponents 10 ft away (medium reach weapons)

weapons.name: Awl Pike
weapons.notes: Extra long spear (16-20 ft); can attack at 20 ft range; cannot be used in areas less than 20 ft long

weapons.name: Bardiche
weapons.notes: Curved single-edged blade on pole; can be set against charge

weapons.name: Bec de Corbin
weapons.notes: Combination of hammer and spike; +1 vs. plate armor

weapons.name: Bill (Bill-Hook)
weapons.notes: Can unhorse mounted opponents; -1 to hit but +1d4 damage vs. mounted

weapons.name: Bohemian Earspoon
weapons.notes: Four-bladed head; thrusting polearm; similar to spear but more damage

weapons.name: Corseque
weapons.notes: Triple-pointed head; can catch and disarm weapons (DEX check at -2)

weapons.name: Fauchard
weapons.notes: Curved cutting blade; can attack opponents trying to disengage

weapons.name: Fauchard-Fork
weapons.notes: Combination fauchard and fork; versatile

weapons.name: Glaive
weapons.notes: Single-edged curved blade; good slashing weapon

weapons.name: Glaive-Guisarme
weapons.notes: Combination glaive and guisarme; very versatile

weapons.name: Guisarme
weapons.notes: Hooked cutting blade; can drag opponents off horses

weapons.name: Guisarme-Voulge
weapons.notes: Combination weapon; heavy

weapons.name: Halberd
weapons.notes: Most versatile polearm; axe + spike + hook; +1 vs. mounted

weapons.name: Hook Fauchard
weapons.notes: Can grab and hold opponents; grapple attempt at no penalty

weapons.name: Lucern Hammer
weapons.notes: Hammer head with spike; +2 damage vs. plate armors

weapons.name: Man Catcher
weapons.notes: Captures without damage; opponent must save vs. paralyzation or be caught; STR 18 check to break free

weapons.name: Military Fork
weapons.notes: Two-tined fork on pole; can set against charge

weapons.name: Partisan
weapons.notes: Broad spearhead with side flanges; +1 to hit vs. mounted; set against charge for double damage

weapons.name: Ranseur
weapons.notes: Triple-pronged blade; can disarm (DEX check at +2)

weapons.name: Spetum
weapons.notes: Main spike with lateral blades; good vs. mounted; set against charge

weapons.name: Spontoon (Esponton)
weapons.notes: Officer's weapon; cross-shaped blade; can set against charge

weapons.name: Trident
weapons.notes: Three-pronged; favored weapon of aquatic races; can catch and hold weapons

weapons.name: Voulge
weapons.notes: Cleaving blade on pole; can set against charge

exotic_and_unusual_weapons.description: Unusual weapons from various cultures that require special proficiency

weapons.name: Aklys (throwing club)
weapons.notes: Weighted club on cord; returns to thrower; takes 1 segment to retrieve

weapons.name: Atlatl (dart thrower)
weapons.notes: Lever arm that extends spear throwing; use only with darts or light spears; requires separate proficiency

weapons.name: Bec de Faucon
weapons.notes: One-handed polearm; falcon's beak hammer head; good vs. heavy armor

weapons.name: Bola
weapons.notes: Three weighted balls on cord; entangles target; medium or smaller target must save vs. paralyzation or fall prone

weapons.name: Chakram
weapons.notes: Metal ring with sharpened edge; can be thrown; can hit multiple targets in a line (proficiency required for multiple targets)

weapons.name: Crook (shepard's)
weapons.notes: Can trip or drag opponents; STR vs. STR check to trip; can hook ankles for prone

weapons.name: Da (Vietnamese staff)
weapons.notes: Bladed staff; deals more damage than quarterstaff; requires martial arts training

weapons.name: Footbow
weapons.notes: Powerful bow drawn with feet while lying down; slow to load but very powerful; requires Weapon Proficiency and acrobatics

weapons.name: Gnomish Crossbow (large)
weapons.notes: Oversized crossbow built by gnomes; fires large bolts; -2 to hit for non-gnomes due to design

weapons.name: Halfling Sling Staff
weapons.notes: Sling attached to short staff; increases range and power; favored by halflings; +1 to hit for halflings

weapons.name: Hook, Grappling
weapons.notes: Used to catch ledges, ropes; can be used as a weapon; attached to 50 ft rope

weapons.name: Jo Stick (Jitte)
weapons.notes: Small rod with side bar; can catch blades; disarm opponent on natural 20; Eastern weapon

weapons.name: Kau Sin Ke (chain whip)
weapons.notes: Multi-section chain weapon; attack ignores shield; requires martial arts proficiency; Eastern weapon

weapons.name: Khopesh
weapons.notes: Egyptian sickle-sword; can hook behind shields (+2 vs. shield AC bonuses)

weapons.name: Kukri
weapons.notes: Inwardly curved knife; favored by Gurkha-type fighters; concealable

weapons.name: Lasso
weapons.notes: Rope loop to capture; hit vs. AC 10 (modified by Dex); target may be ridden down

weapons.name: Lasso (horseback)
weapons.notes: Used from horseback; requires Riding proficiency and Lasso proficiency

weapons.name: Net
weapons.notes: Entangles target; save vs. paralyzation or entangled (halved movement, -2 to attacks); cut with bladed weapon (AC 6) or STR 17+ check

weapons.name: Nunchaku
weapons.notes: Two sections connected by chain; +2 to disarm attempts; Eastern weapon

weapons.name: Rope Dart
weapons.notes: Dart on long rope; used for striking and entangling; complex weapon requiring extensive training

weapons.name: Sai
weapons.notes: Three-pronged truncheon; can catch and disarm weapons on natural 20 or total of 20; Eastern weapon

weapons.name: Sap (blackjack)
weapons.notes: Weighted leather bag; used for non-lethal knockout; sneak attack (backstab) knocks unconscious on max damage

weapons.name: Scourge
weapons.notes: Multi-tailed whip with weighted ends; used for punishment; ignores shields

weapons.name: Shuriken (throwing star)
weapons.notes: Flat star-shaped throwing weapon; multiple thrown (3 per round); Eastern weapon

weapons.name: Sodegarami (sleeve catcher)
weapons.notes: Spiked ring on pole; used to catch clothing and hold criminals; can immobilize without killing; Eastern weapon

weapons.name: Tetsubo (iron club)
weapons.notes: Massive iron-studded club; requires STR 16+; +2 damage vs. armor; Eastern weapon

weapons.name: Tonfa
weapons.notes: Side-handle baton; can be used for defense and offense simultaneously; +1 AC when wielded

weapons.name: Whip
weapons.notes: Attacks at 10-15 ft range; can be used to disarm or trip; entangle like rope on natural 20

eastern_weapons.description: Weapons from Eastern/Oriental cultures (Kara-Tur style settings). Require appropriate proficiency slots.
eastern_weapons.notes: Eastern weapons require 'Oriental Weapon' proficiency groups or specific weapon proficiencies

weapons.name: Bo Stick (Bo)
weapons.notes: Long staff; as quarterstaff but different grip and techniques

weapons.name: Jo Stick (Jo)
weapons.notes: Short staff; like a short quarterstaff

weapons.name: Katana
weapons.notes: Japanese curved sword; masterwork quality; +1 to hit/damage due to superior balance; can be used one or two-handed; two-handed gives +2 damage

weapons.name: Kama
weapons.notes: Small sickle; can be used in pairs; Eastern weapon

weapons.name: Katar (punch dagger)
weapons.notes: H-handled push dagger; held with blade extending from fist; can triple damage on backstab

weapons.name: Naginata
weapons.notes: Japanese glaive; pole weapon with curved blade; effectively a glaive but different attack style

weapons.name: No-Dachi (Odachi)
weapons.notes: Great katana; two-handed massive sword; can cleave through medium creatures

weapons.name: Shikomi-Zue (sword cane)
weapons.notes: Sword hidden in cane; -2 to opponent's surprise checks; first round of combat always surprises

weapons.name: Wakizashi
weapons.notes: Short katana (companion sword); typically paired with katana; excellent balance gives +1 to AC when used in defensive stance

weapons.name: Yari (Japanese Spear)
weapons.notes: Japanese spear; can be set against charge

weapons.name: Yumi (Japanese Bow)
weapons.notes: Asymmetrical longbow; longer range than composite bow but same damage; can be fired from horseback

siege_weapons.description: Large military weapons used in battles and sieges. Require crews to operate.

weapons.name: Ballista
weapons.notes: Giant crossbow mounted on a stand; fires large bolts; can target single creatures or crew inside fortifications; bolt deals 3d6 damage, saves at -2

weapons.name: Catapult (light)
weapons.notes: Lighter catapult; 10-ft radius impact zone; all in zone take damage; saves vs. breath weapon for half

weapons.name: Catapult (heavy)
weapons.notes: Heavy catapult; 20-ft radius zone; used for battering gates and walls; 25 structural points of damage vs. walls

weapons.name: Mangonnel
weapons.notes: Arm-sling type catapult; can hurl burning pitch (incendiary, start fires) or disease-laden materials; 15-ft radius zone

weapons.name: Trebuchet
weapons.notes: Counterweight siege engine; most powerful catapult; can throw 200-lb stones; wall sections take 50 structural points; 30-ft radius zone

weapons.name: Battering Ram
weapons.notes: Deals 1d6 per 2 crew members (max 6d6) to a door or wall section per hit; gates: 3 to 10 successful hits to breach; requires cover to protect crew

weapons.name: Bolt Thrower (Onager)
weapons.notes: Smaller, more mobile ballista variant; fires large bolts; can be horse-drawn; bolt deals 2d8 damage

weapons.name: Crossbow (heavy siege)
weapons.notes: Oversized siege crossbow; +3 to hit vs. armored targets; fires large bolts; one operator aims while second winds the winch

firearms_primitive.description: Very early primitive firearms for campaign settings where such technology exists (optional)
firearms_primitive.notes: These are rare, expensive, and unreliable. Use only in campaigns where firearms technology exists.

weapons.name: Hand Cannon (primitive)
weapons.notes: Primitive handheld firearm; misfires on 1-3 (out of 20); takes 4 rounds to reload; deafens shooter for 1 round; no proficiency bonus available

weapons.name: Bombard (cannon)
weapons.notes: Very early bronze cannon; 1 shot per 6 rounds; misfires on 1-2 (may explode); 30-ft radius area effect vs. troops; primarily siege weapon

chain_and_flail_variants.description: Additional flail and chain weapon variants

weapons.name: Ball and Chain
weapons.notes: Flail-like with longer chain; ignores shield; can wrap around weapon (20% chance to cause opponent to drop weapon on hit)

weapons.name: Dire Flail
weapons.notes: Double-headed flail; both heads can strike simultaneously (attack at -2 for additional attack per round)

weapons.name: Heavy Flail (military)
weapons.notes: Military grade flail; +2 damage vs. chain armor; always ignores shield AC bonus

weapons.name: Horseman's Flail
weapons.notes: Single-handed flail for cavalry use; same as flail but lighter and faster; can be used one-handed

bows_and_projectile_variants.description: Additional bow and ranged weapon types

weapons.name: Composite Bow (medium)
weapons.notes: Between short and long composite bow in size; usable from horseback; STR 13+ required for normal use

weapons.name: Crossbow (hand/wrist)
weapons.notes: Worn on wrist; hands free otherwise; fires small bolts (1d3 dmg); difficult to reload (2 rounds with both hands free); concealable

weapons.name: Crossbow (heavy repeating)
weapons.notes: Magazine-fed crossbow with 5-bolt magazine; fires every other round without reloading; reload magazine takes 1 round after all 5 bolts fired

weapons.name: Crossbow (light repeating)
weapons.notes: Lighter version of repeating crossbow; 3-bolt magazine; fire 1 per round without reloading

weapons.name: Pellet Bow
weapons.notes: Bow designed to fire clay pellets or stones; cheap ammunition; non-lethal on minimum damage (unconscious instead of dead)

weapons.name: Stonebow (Prodd)
weapons.notes: Crossbow designed for stone or clay pellets; cheaper ammunition; used for hunting small game

improvised_weapons.description: Using objects not designed as weapons. Always -2 to hit; proficiency cannot be taken.

examples.name: Chair
examples.notes: Breaks on natural 1; can be used to push back opponent

examples.name: Bottle (full)
examples.notes: Breaks on successful hit; shards cause 1 additional damage

examples.name: Torch (improvised)
examples.notes: Sets combustibles aflame; 50% chance of going out on hit

examples.name: Table leg
examples.notes: Equivalent to a club; may splinter on natural 1

examples.name: Belt buckle
examples.notes: Used as a flail; extremely short reach

examples.name: Rock/Stone
examples.notes: Thrown or used as a club; range 10/20/30 when thrown

examples.name: Wooden stake
examples.notes: Stakes driven through vampire hearts must be done to immobilized vampire; 2d4+2 damage

examples.name: Pitchfork
examples.notes: Functions as a trident or military fork when used as weapon

examples.name: Broken bottle
examples.notes: Jagged glass; causes bleeding (1 HP per round until bandaged)

materials_and_special_construction.description: Special materials that affect weapon properties and costs

materials.name: Cold Iron
materials.notes: Iron mined without fire processes; affects fairies and some demons; required to bypass certain creature immunities
materials.mechanical_effect: As normal weapon but counts as magical vs. fey and some demons

materials.name: Silver
materials.notes: Silver weapons required to harm lycanthropes and some undead; -1 damage due to softness
materials.mechanical_effect: -1 damage; can harm lycanthropes and some creatures immune to normal weapons

materials.name: Adamantite
materials.notes: Super-hard metal; extremely rare; weapons count as +3 for purposes of what they can hit
materials.mechanical_effect: +1 to hit; can harm creatures requiring magical weapons to hit

materials.name: Mithral
materials.notes: Elven metal; lighter than steel; weapons weigh half as much
materials.mechanical_effect: Half weight; +1 to hit (lighter = faster); elven quality

materials.name: Darkwood
materials.notes: Magical wood from certain forests; lighter and tougher than normal wood
materials.mechanical_effect: Half weight for wooden components; +1 to saves vs. breakage

materials.name: Bone/Ivory
materials.notes: Primitive weapons; more fragile but readily available

weapon_quality_grades.notes: Poorly made; -1 to hit; breaks on critical fumble
Well made; no breakage on fumble
Master crafted; saves at +1 vs. breakage
+1 to hit (not magical); exceptional quality; eligible for enchantment
+1 to hit and saves; racial craftsmanship; may have minor non-magical properties
